title: BERT: Context from Attention
concept: BERT replaced LSTMs with Transformers, using self-attention to capture bidirectional context far more effectively and parallelly.

exercises.prompt: How does BERT understand the context of a word in a sentence?

options.text: By using self-attention to look at all words at the same time
By memorizing a dictionary of definitions

exercises.explain: BERT uses Transformers and self-attention to look at all words in a sentence at once, allowing it to capture context from both directions simultaneously.

cards.title: Reading Both Ways
cards.body: Have you ever tried to understand a sentence but got confused because a word has multiple meanings? Older AI read words one by one, like a slow train. BERT changed the game by looking at the entire sentence at once, capturing clues from all directions!

cards.title: BERT and Attention
cards.body: BERT is an AI model that replaced older recurrent networks (LSTMs) with a Transformer architecture. Instead of processing words one-by-one, BERT uses **self-attention** to look at all words in a sentence at the same time, capturing context from both left and right directions simultaneously.

cards.title: The Spotlight Table
cards.body: Imagine reading a mystery book where you can only read left-to-right, one word at a time, forgetting the start by the time you reach the end. That is an LSTM. Now, imagine laying the entire page flat on a table and shining a spotlight on all relevant clues simultaneously. That is BERT's self-attention!

cards.title: Context in Action
cards.body: Take the sentence: 'The bank of the river was muddy.' \n\nAn LSTM reads 'bank' before it ever sees 'river', making it hard to guess the meaning early on. BERT looks at 'bank' and 'river' at the same time, instantly realizing 'bank' means land, not a place for money!

cards.title: It's Not Just Memory
cards.body: Many people think BERT is better just because it has a bigger memory. In reality, it is better because of its architecture! By throwing away step-by-step loops and using parallel attention, BERT understands relationships between words far more accurately.

cards.title: LSTM vs. BERT
cards.body: See how the architecture design changes the way AI processes language.

right.text: Processes all words at once. Captures full context instantly.

left.text: Processes words one-by-one. Easy to forget early context.

exercises.prompt: How does BERT analyze a sentence differently than an LSTM?

options.text: It processes text one word at a time, from left to right.
It processes all words in a sentence simultaneously using self-attention.
It ignores words that have multiple meanings to save time.
It translates sentences into another language before reading them.

exercises.explain: LSTMs process text sequentially (one word after another), while BERT's Transformer architecture processes all words at the same time using attention.

exercises.prompt: True or False: BERT processes sentences word-by-word in a single, unchangeable direction.
exercises.explain: Unlike LSTMs, which read step-by-step, BERT processes all words in parallel using self-attention.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe how BERT analyzes word relationships simultaneously.
exercises.sentence: BERT replaces step-by-step loops with self-_______ to process sentences in parallel.
exercises.explain: BERT uses self-attention to look at all parts of a sentence at the same time, capturing full bidirectional context.

exercises.prompt: Which statement is an accurate, AI-generated explanation of BERT's core advantage?

options.text: BERT uses bidirectional self-attention to analyze all words in a sentence at once, replacing the sequential processing of LSTMs.
BERT works by translating text into binary code and using traditional decision trees to find matching words.
BERT processes sentences by reading them backwards first, then forwards, and averaging the two results.

exercises.explain: The first option is generated by an AI explaining BERT's mechanism. The other two are incorrect descriptions of how BERT works.

exercises.prompt: Why is BERT's self-attention architecture generally faster and more powerful for understanding context than older LSTM networks?

options.text: LSTMs process entire sentences simultaneously, while BERT reads one word at a time.
BERT relies on recurrences and loops to remember words from the past.
BERT uses self-attention to process all words at once, avoiding the slow step-by-step processing of LSTMs.
BERT processes sentences slower than LSTMs because it has to look both ways.

exercises.explain: BERT uses bidirectional self-attention to process all words in a sentence simultaneously (parallel processing), making it faster and better at understanding context than the step-by-step nature of LSTMs.
